plugin_desc: Alcatel-Lucent (Nokia) TiMOS-specific SNMP collection for switches. In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects TIMETRA-CHASSIS-MIB OIDs for device health: CPU utilization, system memory pool (used/free bytes), hardware temperature sensors, fan and power-supply status. Memory utilization is computed from used/(used+free); fan/psu states (TmnxDeviceState) are normalized to the unified 1=normal convention.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the Alcatel-Lucent (TiMOS) device control plane (TIMETRA-CHASSIS-MIB sgiCpuUsage), normalized to a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_used
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of bytes currently in use by the Alcatel-Lucent (TiMOS) system memory (TIMETRA-CHASSIS-MIB sgiMemoryUsed). It helps administrators understand absolute memory consumption and identify when the device is under memory pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_free
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of free bytes available in the Alcatel-Lucent (TiMOS) system memory (TIMETRA-CHASSIS-MIB sgiMemoryAvailable). Together with the used bytes it determines overall memory utilization; a persistently low free value indicates memory pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the Alcatel-Lucent (TiMOS) device, computed as used / (used + free) * 100. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the temperature reading of each Alcatel-Lucent (TiMOS) hardware sensor (TIMETRA-CHASSIS-MIB tmnxHwTemperature), in degrees Celsius. Abnormally high temperatures may indicate fan failure, blocked airflow, or excessive ambient heat, and can trigger thermal protection or hardware damage if left unaddressed.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the operational state of each Alcatel-Lucent (TiMOS) cooling fan (TIMETRA-CHASSIS-MIB TmnxDeviceState), normalized to the unified convention where 1 means normal and any other value indicates an abnormal/fault state. A non-normal state indicates a cooling fault that can quickly lead to overheating and should be investigated immediately.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the operational state of each Alcatel-Lucent (TiMOS) power supply unit (TIMETRA-CHASSIS-MIB TmnxDeviceState), normalized to the unified convention where 1 means normal and any other value indicates an abnormal/fault state. A non-normal state signals a power redundancy or hardware fault and must be remediated promptly.
